basic_details.job_title: UCIL Professionals Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 19 Posts
basic_details.post_name: Regular posts in Group-A and Group-B disciplines
basic_details.short_information: Uranium Corporation of India Limited (UCIL) Recruitment of Professionals 2026 Notification has been released on the official website www.uraniumcorp.in. A total of 19 vacancies have been announced through Advertisement No. UCIL-09-2026 for various Group-A and Group-B regular posts in multiple disciplines.

important_dates.raw: Online Registration Opens: 20 April 2026 - 10:00 AM Last Date to Apply Online: 19 May 2026 - 11:59 PM

application_fee.details: General (UR) / EWS / OBC (NCL): Rs. 500/- SC / ST / PwBD / Female / Internal UCIL Candidates: NIL | Fee must be paid online only through the UCIL Recruitment Portal at the time of application. A copy of the fee acknowledgement/receipt must be uploaded. Fee once paid is non-refundable. Internal UCIL candidates are exempted from payment of application fees.

vacancy_details.Qualification: Qualified Chartered Accountant (ICAI) or Cost Accountant (ICMAI). Experience: Post-qualification experience in Accounts Department of a PSU/large concern, preferably in Mining & Processing Industry. Exposure to Works Accounting, Project Accounting, Treasury Management, and computerised environment preferred.

vacancy_details.Qualification & Experience Notes: Degree in any discipline (including Engineering) + 2-year full time PG Degree/Diploma in Personnel Mgmt/Labour & Social Welfare/IR/HRM/Training & Development OR MBA with specialisation in Personnel Mgmt/HRD. Experience: Post-qualification relevant experience in PSU/large organisations in Labour, Personnel, IR & General Administration.

Same PG qualification as above (Personnel/HRD/IR). Experience: Relevant experience managing industrial security, working in Officer/Executive cadre in Govt./PSE/Private organisation; knowledge of fire-fighting, Estate Administration, and computer operations.

Degree in Law from a recognised university/institution. PG qualification in Commercial Laws/Labour Laws will be an added qualification. Experience: Post-qualification experience dealing with legal matters in industries or standing practice as a lawyer.

B.E/B.Tech in Mining Engineering or equivalent + Second Class Mines Manager Certificate of Competency (Unrestricted) for metalliferous mines. Experience: Post-qualification working experience in production line of large mechanised underground/opencast metal mines with modern equipment. Proven experience leading a multidisciplinary team; knowledge of mining rules/regulations and regulatory authority dealings essential.

Degree in Civil Engineering or equivalent from a recognised university/institution. Experience: Post-qualification working experience preferably in structural/industrial design of buildings/structures, dams, and drawing up contracts.

Degree in Engineering with 1-year Diploma in Materials Management OR Degree in any discipline + 2-year PG Degree/Diploma in Materials Management OR MBA with specialisation in Materials Management. Experience: Post-qualification experience in stores & purchase/material management/supply chain management.
MBBS Degree recognised by the Indian Medical Council. Experience: Post-qualification relevant experience in a reputed hospital. PG Degree will be an added qualification.

Degree in Environmental Engineering OR Degree in any branch of Engineering OR Ph.D in Organic/Inorganic Chemistry + PG Diploma OR full-time Certificate Course in Environmental Engineering/Environmental Sciences. Experience: Post-qualification experience in Environment Management in a large and reputed organisation. Knowledge of Indian Environmental Legislation, EIA/EMP, Environment Audit Process, Waste Disposal Management. Mining/processing industry experience preferred.

Degree in Industrial Engineering or equivalent OR Graduate in Engineering + PG qualification in Industrial Engineering from a University/recognised institution. Experience: Post-qualification experience in the relevant area in any PSU/large organisation.

Diploma (Full-time) in Mining Engineering OR Diploma in Mining and Mine Surveying + valid Mines Foreman Certificate of Competency for metalliferous mines (unrestricted) issued by DGMS + valid First Aid certificate. Experience: Post-qualification relevant experience in underground metalliferous mines.

salary_details.pay_scale: IDA Grade (E-0 to E-6) - as per post

selection_process: The selection process may include a Written Test / Trade Test / Psychometric Test / Group Exercise / Personal Interview or combinations thereof
depending on the response received for each post. Only shortlisted candidates will be called for further stages of the selection process.

PwBD) will be done at the time of Written Test/Group Discussion/Personal Interview. UCIL reserves the right to cancel/restrict/enlarge/modify the recruitment and selection process without assigning any reason. Candidates applying for multiple posts must submit separate applications and pay separate fees for each post. Management reserves the right to offer a lower position based on interview performance.
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only through the UCIL Job Portal. No other mode of application will be accepted.
1. Read the advertisement carefully: Go through the detailed instructions available on the UCIL Recruitment Portal at www.uraniumcorp.in before filling in the application.

2. Register and fill the online form: Visit https://ucil.gov.in/jobportal/public/index.php and complete the online application form. Fill in all personal, educational, and work experience details accurately. Ensure category, post applied for, and PwBD status are correct — these cannot be changed after submission.

3. Pay the application fee: Candidates belonging to General/UR/EWS/OBC-NCL categories must pay Rs. 500/- online. SC/ST/PwBD/Female/Internal UCIL candidates are exempted. Upload the fee acknowledgement copy.

4. Upload required documents: Upload self-attested copies of — Class 10th certificate, Category certificate (SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS), PwBD certificate (if applicable), mark sheets and degree certificates for educational qualifications, work experience certificates (offer letter, appointment letter, latest pay slip, pay continuity documents), salary certificate/Form-16 (for private sector candidates), company annual report for turnover proof, and NOC from present employer (Govt./PSU/CPSE candidates applying without proper channel).

5. Final submission: Review all entries carefully before final submission. After submission, no changes to mailing address, email ID, category, or post will be entertained. Keep registered mobile number and email active throughout the recruitment process.
6. Internal UCIL candidates: Must apply online/offline and forward the hard copy of the application through proper channel. Failure to do so may result in rejection of candidature.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies in UCIL Recruitment of Professionals 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: A total of 19 vacancies have been notified under UCIL Recruitment of Professionals 2026, Advertisement No. UCIL‑09‑2026, across Accounts, Personnel/HR, Legal, Security & Administration, Mining, Civil, Purchase, Medical, Environmental Engineering, Industrial Engineering, and Foreman (Mining) disciplines.

faq.question: Q2. What are the online application dates for UCIL Recruitment of Professionals 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: Online registration opens on 20 April 2026 at 10:00 AM and the last date to apply online is 19 May 2026 up to 11:59 PM through the UCIL Job Portal.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application fee for UCIL Recruitment 2026 (Professionals)?
faq.answer: Ans: General/UR, EWS and OBC (NCL) candidates must pay Rs. 500 as a non‑refundable application fee, while SC, ST, PwBD, Female and Internal UCIL candidates are exempted from paying any fee.

faq.question: Q4. What are the pay scales for the advertised UCIL Professional posts in 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: The posts are in IDA Grades E‑0 to E‑6, with basic pay ranges from Rs. 30,000–1,20,000 (Foreman, E‑0) up to Rs. 90,000–2,40,000 (Dy. General Manager, E‑6), plus DA, perks, accommodation, medical and other benefits as per UCIL rules.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit and relaxation for UCIL Professional posts 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: The maximum age varies by post (generally 30–48 years), with relaxations of 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC (NCL), 10 years for PwBD (UR/EWS), and additional combined relaxations for PwBD–SC/ST/OBC and J&K domicile candidates; internal UCIL candidates have no upper age limit.

faq.question: Q6. What are the key educational qualifications for Accounts and Personnel/HR posts in UCIL Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: Accounts posts require a qualified Chartered Accountant (ICAI) or Cost Accountant (ICMAI) with relevant post‑qualification experience, while Personnel/HR posts require a degree in any discipline plus a 2‑year full‑time PG degree/diploma in Personnel Management/Labour & Social Welfare/IR/HRM/Training & Development or MBA with HR/PM specialisation.

faq.question: Q7. What are the core technical qualifications for Mining, Civil, Purchase and Medical posts?
faq.answer: Ans: Mining posts need BE/B.Tech in Mining plus a valid Second Class Mines Manager’s Certificate (metalliferous); Civil requires a degree in Civil Engineering; Purchase requires Engineering degree with Materials Management diploma or degree plus PG in Materials/MBA (Materials); Medical Services require MBBS recognised by NMC with relevant hospital experience, with PG degree as an added advantage.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for UCIL Recruitment of Professionals 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: Selection may include a Written Test, Trade Test, Psychometric Test, Group Exercise and/or Personal Interview, singly or in combination, depending on the post and number of applicants, followed by verification of original documents; only shortlisted candidates will be called for the next stage.

faq.question: Q9. How can candidates apply online for UCIL Professional posts 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: Candidates must apply only through the UCIL Job Portal at https://ucil.gov.in/jobportal/public/index.php, fill all personal, qualification and experience details, upload required documents and fee receipt, and submit the form online before 19 May 2026; no other mode of application will be accepted.

faq.question: Q10. Are internal UCIL employees and multi‑post applicants subject to any special conditions?
faq.answer: Ans: Internal UCIL candidates are exempt from age and fee limits but must apply through proper channel, while candidates wishing to apply for more than one post must submit separate online applications and fees for each post; management may also offer a lower position than applied for based on performance.
